name: Yucatan Mixed-Spice Paste
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast unpeeled garlic on a griddle or heavy skillet over medium heat for about 15 minutes, turning frequently until blackened in spots and very soft inside.
2. Remove the garlic from the heat and let it cool. Slip off the skins.
3. If using whole spices, place oregano, black peppercorns, allspice, cloves, and cumin seeds in a mortar or spice grinder and pulverize thoroughly. Transfer to a small bowl and add salt. If using ground spices, combine them with salt in a small bowl.
4. Finely mince the garlic and sprinkle it with some of the spice mixture.
5. Use the back of a spoon to work the minced garlic into the spice mixture until it forms a smooth paste.
6. Add the remaining spices to the paste, then incorporate the vinegar and flour. Work everything together until well combined.
7. Scrape the paste into a small jar, cover tightly, and let it stand in the refrigerator for several hours or preferably overnight before using. The paste will keep refrigerated for several weeks.
Makes about 3 tablespoons.
